query: behavioral patterns associated with mental retardation vs autism spectrum disorders in young children

retrieved_docs.title: Asperger Syndrome -- History and Physical -- Decrease in Symptoms into Adulthood
retrieved_docs.content: Studies have indicated that ASD symptoms generally diminish as individuals with ASD transition into adulthood. This symptom reduction is more pronounced among those with average or above-average intelligence compared to individuals with ASD and intellectual disability. Social challenges, perceptual deficits, difficulties with nonverbal communication, and restricted and repetitive behaviors are reported to remain stable over time. [35]

retrieved_docs.title: Asperger Syndrome -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Autism spectrum disorders (ASD) encompass a range of neurodevelopmental conditions characterized by diverse degrees and manifestations. Typically emerging in early childhood, these disorders are marked by challenges in social communication and interaction and behavioral patterns that involve restricted interests and repetitive behaviors. [4] [5] The changes made to the classification of ASD in DSM-5 sparked controversy regarding the loss of the distinct Asperger identity. This shift in classification continues to be a topic of debate within the literature, as discussions revolve around the formulation and inclusion of AS within the broader ASD framework. [6] [7] Given the extensive historical background of AS, its distinct semiotics, and the relatively characteristic clinical presentations, specialists still utilize this diagnosis as a subtype of ASD characterized by the absence of language delay and a normal or above-average IQ. [6]

retrieved_docs.title: Autism Spectrum Disorder -- Epidemiology
retrieved_docs.content: Changes over time in how autism is characterized confound epidemiological estimates of the prevalence of ASD. The term "autism" was coined by Eugen Bleuler in 1911 to describe the extreme social withdrawal and self-centeredness sometimes present in patients with schizophrenia. Leo Kanner used the term autism in 1943 to describe children who had language abnormalities, then later hypothesized that extreme self-isolation and insistence on sameness were core features of the syndrome. [14] [15] In 1944, Hans Asperger described children with social deficits and above-average language skills. [14] Asperger syndrome would eventually be included with autistic disorder, Rett syndrome, childhood disintegrative disorder, and pervasive developmental disorder not otherwise specified as subtypes of pervasive developmental disorder in the DSM-IV, published in 1994. [16] Historians of child psychiatry have since brought to light Asperger collusion with the Nazis in identifying children for child euthanasia, and his work has been discredited. [17]

retrieved_docs.title: Autism Spectrum Disorder -- Epidemiology
retrieved_docs.content: Questioning the validity of childhood disintegrative disorder and recognizing Rett syndrome as a separate entity, in 2013, the DSM-V replaced pervasive developmental disorder with autism spectrum disorder and abandoned the not-otherwise specified designation. Today's autism spectrum disorder is defined by a single set of categorical criteria within the domains of communication deficits and repetitive behaviors. [18] ASD has a heterogeneous prevalence globally, and rates in each country are influenced by diagnostic methods, the populations surveyed, and the resources available. [19] One of the first prevalence estimates for autism was published in 1967 and was based on a study of English children aged 8, 9, and 10. The survey showed that 4.5 children per 10,000 had "autistic conditions of early childhood." [20] A recent global meta-analysis showed a pooled prevalence of ASD of 1.01% in North America, 0.73% in Europe, and 0.41% in Asia. The highest prevalence was in the United States (1.12%), Sweden (0.90%), and Denmark (0.73%). The lowest prevalence was in Taiwan (0.11%), France (0.32%), and China (0.42%). The global point-prevalence of ASD increased from 0.25% from 1994 to 1999 to 0.99% from 2015 to 2019. [21]

retrieved_docs.title: Asperger Syndrome -- Introduction -- DSM Classification
retrieved_docs.content: In DSM-IV-TR, the symptoms and clinical specifiers for autistic disorder within the pervasive developmental disorders (PDD) category fell into three broad categories: social interaction, communication, and restricted and repetitive behavior. [8] The diagnostic criteria for Asperger syndrome (AS) used to include: [7] Individuals exhibiting at least two symptoms of social impairment and at least one symptom each from the category of communication deficits and restricted, repetitive behaviors (RRB). Delayed or impaired functioning in at least one of the following areas: social interaction, language as used in social communication, or symbolic or imaginative play with onset before the age of three years. Individuals who met the diagnostic criteria for autistic disorder (or another specific PDD) would not meet the criteria for AS. In such cases, the diagnosis of autistic disorder would take precedence.

retrieved_docs.title: Autism Spectrum Disorder -- Treatment / Management -- Focused Intervention Practices for ASD
retrieved_docs.content: Differential reinforcement of alternative, incompatible, or other behavior for academic/pre-academic, adaptive/self-help, challenging/interfering behavior, communication, joint attention, motor, play, school readiness, and social outcomes

retrieved_docs.title: Asperger Syndrome -- History and Physical
retrieved_docs.content: ASD is a heterogeneous, neurodevelopmental disorder encompassing a range of features and impairments. It is characterized by abnormal social communication and interaction; and restricted and repetitive behavior patterns. Symptoms in the early developmental period may not become fully evident until social demands exceed limited capacities. Specifiers in the diagnosis of ASD include the presence of intellectual disability, language impairment, medical or genetic condition, catatonia, and other neurodevelopmental, mental, or behavioral disorders. Each core symptom domain has 3 levels of severity, which are classified based on the severity of symptoms and the required level of support needed to assist with impairments. Separate ratings are necessary for each part (table 1). [15]

retrieved_docs.title: Autism Spectrum Disorder -- Prognosis
retrieved_docs.content: The prognosis of ASD varies significantly among individuals, making it challenging to predict outcomes, especially at the time of diagnosis. While some children may exhibit improvements in core symptoms, 9 out of 10 children diagnosed with ASD who are younger than 6 years of age continued to meet the criteria for ASD a year or more later, but the evidence for this is not robust. [31] Favorable prognostic factors include higher cognitive skills at 2 years of age, participation in earlier intervention services, decreased repetitive behaviors, increased tested verbal intelligence quotient, and the presence of family and community supports. [6] Executive function difficulties are associated with poorer outcomes. [6] ASD is associated with a significantly increased risk of all-cause mortality, including ending one's own life. [32] [33]

retrieved_docs.title: Autism Spectrum Disorder -- Etiology
retrieved_docs.content: Maternal use of serotonin reuptake inhibitors or other antidepressants may be linked to ASD. This association is attenuated when controlled for pre-existing psychiatric conditions, however. [8]

retrieved_docs.title: Autism Spectrum Disorder -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: These disturbances are not better explained by intellectual developmental disorder (intellectual disability) or global developmental delay. Intellectual developmental disorder and ASD frequently co-occur. To make comorbid diagnoses of ASD and intellectual developmental disorder, social communication should be below that expected for the general developmental level.

retrieved_docs.title: Oppositional Defiant Disorder -- Differential Diagnosis -- Autism Spectrum Disorder
retrieved_docs.content: Autism spectrum disorder may present with noncompliance with requests or seeming defiance of authority figures due to social communication deficits, speech development delays, difficulties adjusting behaviors to a new context, restrictive patterns of behavior and interest, and inflexibility. Autism spectrum disorder and oppositional defiant disorder may be diagnosed together. However, determining whether the symptoms attributed to oppositional defiant disorder are better explained by autism spectrum disorder  is essential.

retrieved_docs.content: Fragile X syndrome: This disorder is often associated with intellectual disability and characteristic physical features.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 1607
retrieved_docs.content: Developmental disorders constitute a broad group of conditions that affect neurodevelopmental processes. Within this overarching category, other psychiatric disorders are regarded as a subset, indicating that these psychiatric conditions share developmental origins or characteristics. Moreover, other psychiatric disorders are also classified as a form of رُهاب (phobia), reflecting that some of these conditions present primarily as intense, irrational fears. This dual classification underscores the overlap between developmental psychopathology and specific anxiety‑related disorders, highlighting the need for clinicians to consider developmental factors when diagnosing and managing phobic psychiatric presentations.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 4 -- Community 2800
retrieved_docs.content: Psychological disorder (اضطراب نفسي) is a broad diagnostic category that includes several specific mental health conditions. Autistic disorder (اضطراب توحدي) is classified as a child of psychological disorder, indicating it is a subtype within this larger group. Separation anxiety disorder (اضطراب قلق الانفصال) has a narrower relationship to psychological disorder, further emphasizing its status as a specific form of psychological disorder. Depression (اكتئاب) is also identified as a child of psychological disorder, highlighting its role as another distinct subtype. Recognizing that autistic disorder, separation anxiety disorder, and depression are all subcategories of psychological disorder helps clinicians organize assessment, diagnosis, and treatment strategies within a unified framework of mental health care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2283
retrieved_docs.content: Mental disorder in adolescence is linked to a pathological developmental process, as indicated by the relationship “has_pathological_process.” This connection highlights that adolescent mental disorders arise from abnormal developmental mechanisms, emphasizing the need to consider disrupted developmental pathways when diagnosing and treating these conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2766
retrieved_docs.content: Psychological disorder (اضطراب نفسي) is a broad category of mental health conditions that encompasses more specific disorders such as depression (اكتئاب) and separation‑anxiety disorder (اضطراب قلق الانفصال), each of which is a narrower subtype within the overall class of psychological disorders. Both the general category of psychological disorder and its child conditions—depression and separation‑anxiety disorder—are identified as targets for prevention and control measures, indicating that strategies aimed at preventing onset, reducing severity, and managing symptoms are applicable across the spectrum of these mental health conditions. This hierarchical relationship underscores the importance of applying preventive and control interventions at both the overarching disorder level and the individual disorder level to improve patient outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 34 -- Level 3 -- Community 1489
retrieved_docs.content: Diagnostic imaging is an allowed qualifier for اضطراب نفسي (psychological disorder), اضطراب توحدي (autistic disorder), and اضطراب قلق الانفصال (separation‑anxiety disorder). اضطراب توحدي is classified as a subtype of اضطراب نفسي, and اضطراب قلق الانفصال represents a narrower subcategory within اضطراب نفسي. These relationships indicate that diagnostic imaging may be employed in the evaluation of these mental‑health conditions, and that both autistic disorder and separation‑anxiety disorder are encompassed by the broader category of psychological disorder.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 17 -- Level 3 -- Community 1576
retrieved_docs.content: Anxiety disorders comprise a broad diagnostic category that includes several specific subtypes. One such subtype, Anxiety disorder due to another medical condition, is classified under the related category Anxiety disorder due to known physiological condition and is defined by explicit diagnostic criteria that are incorporated into the overall diagnostic framework for anxiety disorders. Another distinct entity, Other specified transient mental disorders due to conditions classified elsewhere, is recognized as a child category within the anxiety disorders hierarchy. Recognizing these hierarchical relationships and the associated diagnostic criteria is essential for accurate identification and appropriate management of patients whose anxiety symptoms are linked to underlying medical or physiological conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 23 -- Level 3 -- Community 2541
retrieved_docs.content: The term اضطراب نفسي (psychological disorder) functions as a broad diagnostic category that encompasses more specific conditions such as اضطراب توحدي (autistic disorder) and اضطراب قلق الانفصال (separation anxiety disorder). Autistic disorder is recognized as a child or subtype of اضطراب نفسي, while اضطراب قلق الانفصال is described as a narrower relationship within the same overarching category. For each of these entities—اضطراب نفسي, اضطراب توحدي, and اضطراب قلق الانفصال—the qualifier patofyziologie (pathophysiology) is allowed, indicating that discussion of their underlying biological mechanisms is appropriate and can be applied to their description and study.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 28 -- Level 3 -- Community 2561
retrieved_docs.content: Mental disorder (اضطراب نفسي) is the overarching category that encompasses specific conditions such as autism disorder (اضطراب توحدي) and depression (اكتئاب). Autism disorder is identified as a child of the broader mental disorder category, and depression is likewise a child condition under mental disorder. For each of these entities—mental disorder, autism disorder, and depression—prevention and control (prevence a kontrola) are recognized as appropriate and permissible qualifiers, indicating that preventive and control measures are applicable and recommended for managing all three conditions. Implementing such prevention and control strategies across this spectrum of mental health disorders supports comprehensive patient care and aligns with best‑practice clinical guidelines.

retrieved_docs.content: Attention‑deficit is classified as a preadult mental disorder, indicating that it belongs to the spectrum of mental health conditions that typically emerge before adulthood.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 2 -- Community 944
retrieved_docs.content: Other psychiatric disorders are categorized as a subset of developmental disorders, indicating that they fall within the broader group of developmental conditions. Within the category of other psychiatric disorders, specific entities such as اضطراب الكرب التالي للرض and رُهاب are recognized as distinct subtypes. This hierarchical organization shows that اضطراب الكرب التالي للرض and رُهاب are particular forms of other psychiatric disorders, which themselves are encompassed by the larger class of developmental disorders. Understanding this taxonomy helps clinicians appreciate the relationships among these psychiatric conditions and supports more precise diagnosis and management within the context of developmental psychopathology.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 3 -- Community 2628
retrieved_docs.content: Psychological disorder (اضطراب نفسي) serves as a broad diagnostic category that encompasses several more specific conditions. Autism disorder (اضطراب توحدي) is classified as a child of the psychological disorder category, indicating it is a subtype within this broader group. Separation anxiety disorder (اضطراب قلق الانفصال) is described as having a narrower relationship to psychological disorder, further specifying it as a distinct but related subtype. Depression (اكتئاب) is also identified as a child of psychological disorder, highlighting its place within the same overarching classification. Epidemiology is recognized as an allowed qualifier for psychological disorder, autism disorder, separation anxiety disorder, and depression, underscoring the importance of population‑level data and prevalence information for each of these conditions. Additionally, the antidepressant venlafaxine (venlafaxin) is noted as a possible treatment option for autism disorder, suggesting its therapeutic relevance in managing this specific subtype.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 3 -- Community 1600
retrieved_docs.content: Psychological disorder (اضطراب نفسي) serves as a broad category that includes several specific mental health conditions. Autistic disorder (اضطراب توحدي) is classified as a child of psychological disorder, indicating it is a subtype within this larger group. Separation anxiety disorder (اضطراب قلق الانفصال) also falls under psychological disorder, described as having a narrower relationship, meaning it is a more specific form of the broader category. Depression (اكتئاب) is another child of psychological disorder, representing a distinct but related condition. Enzymology is recognized as an allowed qualifier for the overarching category of psychological disorder as well as for each of its specific subtypes—autistic disorder, separation anxiety disorder, and depression—signifying that enzymatic considerations or descriptors are applicable in the classification, study, or description of these mental health disorders.
